name: Belle Baker
abstract: Belle Baker (born Bella Becker; December 25, 1893, in New York City– April 29, 1957, in Los Angeles) was a Jewish American singer, actress, and Vaudeville performer. Popular throughout the 1910s and 1920s, Baker introduced a number of ragtime and torch songs including Irving Berlin's "Blue Skies" and "My Yiddishe Momme".

about_quotes.speaker: Chuck Green
about_quotes.evidence: And they were more like relations to other show people, show folk, you know, like -- men and women, like Harry Richman, the great jazz singer, and that lady -- what's her name? Belle Baker.
